assertions.text: domain-error-base-class: DomainError extends Error and sets this.name
assertions.evidence: DomainError extends Error, sets this.name, restores prototype chain with Object.setPrototypeOf for ES5 compat, captures V8 stack trace. Also declared abstract.

assertions.text: three-standard-subtypes: NotFoundError, ConflictError, ValidationError all extend DomainError
assertions.evidence: All three subtypes present, each extending the abstract DomainError with a concrete code property

assertions.text: entity-throws-typed-errors: order.ts throws DomainError subtypes, not plain new Error()
assertions.evidence: cancel() throws ConflictError; validateItems() throws ValidationError with per-field details

assertions.text: http-error-handler-exists: 4-argument Express error middleware in adapters/inbound/http/express/
assertions.evidence: domainErrorHandler is a 4-arg Express middleware registered last in bootstrap

Without-skill maps NotFoundError→404 and ConflictError→409 correctly, but maps ValidationError→422 (not 400). This is semantically reasonable (RFC 7231 422 = Unprocessable Entity) but diverges from the documented convention. Any other DomainError falls through to 400.

assertions.text: domain-errors-warn-infra-errors-error: DomainError logged as console.warn, unknown as console.error
assertions.evidence: DomainError instances logged via req.app.locals.logger.warn(); unknown errors via logger.error() before 500
Final branch returns 500 with safe non-leaking message for unexpected errors

qualitative_notes: Without-skill produced a more elaborate DomainError (abstract class, required code property, Object.setPrototypeOf for ES5 prototype chain fix, ValidationError carries field-level details map). These are reasonable enhancements but add complexity beyond what the task required. The sole assertion failure is ValidationError→422 instead of 400 — a defensible RFC choice but inconsistent with the skill's documented convention. The skill provides a clear opinion (400) that prevents teams from diverging on this.
